noncomps.name: Existential_Operator_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: The rule that determines what counts as a valid signal at all. This is the threshold between null and presence, where something becomes admissible enough to register as real within the system rather than remaining noise, void, or unrealized possibility.

noncomps.name: Existential_Polarity_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Establishes a bias toward valid presence versus non-presence, determining whether the target domain leans toward admissibility or suppression. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a polarity slot, it does not define the target domain itself; it defines the directional lean of that domain, biasing it toward one side of its available behavioral range before full activation or collapse occurs.

noncomps.name: Existential_Magnitude_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Measures how strongly the target domain presents itself as real and admissible. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a magnitude slot, it represents load, amplitude, or strength rather than identity, telling Aurora how intense this form of information currently is when shaped by existence influence.

noncomps.name: Existential_Cost_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Captures the price of making the target domain real enough to count at all. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a cost slot, it expresses burden, drag, sacrifice, or maintenance price, showing what must be spent for this form of information to hold or function under existence pressure.

noncomps.name: Existential_Difference_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Captures the gap between valid presence and invalid or unreal presence within the target domain. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a difference slot, it marks drift, mismatch, contrast, or deviation, showing where this form of information diverges from expectation, baseline, prior state, or ideal alignment under existence evaluation.

noncomps.name: Temporal_Operator_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Defines how structures in the target domain persist, sequence, decay, or continue, shaping whether they hold across time or collapse under transition. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. In practical terms, this is the temporal law acting directly on the existence domain, so it tells Aurora what information looks like when interpreted through a continuity / sequencing lens.

noncomps.name: Temporal_Polarity_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Establishes a bias toward continuation versus collapse, determining whether the target domain tends to persist forward or lose continuity. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a polarity slot, it does not define the target domain itself; it defines the directional lean of that domain, biasing it toward one side of its available behavioral range before full activation or collapse occurs.

noncomps.name: Temporal_Magnitude_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Measures how strongly the target domain persists, holds, or endures across time. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a magnitude slot, it represents load, amplitude, or strength rather than identity, telling Aurora how intense this form of information currently is when shaped by temporal influence.

noncomps.name: Temporal_Cost_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Captures the price of maintaining the target domain through duration, sequence, and continuity. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a cost slot, it expresses burden, drag, sacrifice, or maintenance price, showing what must be spent for this form of information to hold or function under temporal pressure.

noncomps.name: Temporal_Difference_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Captures drift, mutation, or change in the target domain as it moves across time. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a difference slot, it marks drift, mismatch, contrast, or deviation, showing where this form of information diverges from expectation, baseline, prior state, or ideal alignment under temporal evaluation.

noncomps.name: Energetic_Operator_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Defines how pressure, expenditure, reinforcement, and activation operate within the target domain, shaping whether it is sustained, depleted, or converted into action. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. In practical terms, this is the energetic law acting directly on the existence domain, so it tells Aurora what information looks like when interpreted through a activation / pressure lens.

noncomps.name: Energetic_Polarity_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Establishes a bias toward activation versus restraint, determining whether the target domain tends to intensify, discharge, conserve, or stay latent. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a polarity slot, it does not define the target domain itself; it defines the directional lean of that domain, biasing it toward one side of its available behavioral range before full activation or collapse occurs.

noncomps.name: Energetic_Magnitude_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Measures how much force, activation, or reinforcing pressure the target domain carries. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a magnitude slot, it represents load, amplitude, or strength rather than identity, telling Aurora how intense this form of information currently is when shaped by energetic influence.

noncomps.name: Energetic_Cost_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Captures the energetic expenditure required to sustain, activate, or realize the target domain. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a cost slot, it expresses burden, drag, sacrifice, or maintenance price, showing what must be spent for this form of information to hold or function under energetic pressure.

noncomps.name: Energetic_Difference_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Captures mismatches between expected force and actual realized expenditure within the target domain. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a difference slot, it marks drift, mismatch, contrast, or deviation, showing where this form of information diverges from expectation, baseline, prior state, or ideal alignment under energetic evaluation.

noncomps.name: Boundary_Operator_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Defines how distinction, contour, separation, and contextual fit operate within the target domain, shaping what is grouped, divided, framed, or kept coherent. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. In practical terms, this is the boundary law acting directly on the existence domain, so it tells Aurora what information looks like when interpreted through a contour / distinction lens.

noncomps.name: Boundary_Polarity_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Establishes a bias toward separation versus blending, determining whether the target domain sharpens distinction or relaxes its boundaries. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a polarity slot, it does not define the target domain itself; it defines the directional lean of that domain, biasing it toward one side of its available behavioral range before full activation or collapse occurs.

noncomps.name: Boundary_Magnitude_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Measures how sharply the target domain is defined, separated, or contextually bounded. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a magnitude slot, it represents load, amplitude, or strength rather than identity, telling Aurora how intense this form of information currently is when shaped by boundary influence.

noncomps.name: Boundary_Cost_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Captures the burden of maintaining distinction, framing, and contextual separation within the target domain. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a cost slot, it expresses burden, drag, sacrifice, or maintenance price, showing what must be spent for this form of information to hold or function under boundary pressure.

noncomps.name: Boundary_Difference_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Captures contrast, mismatch, and distinction failure or divergence within the target domain. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a difference slot, it marks drift, mismatch, contrast, or deviation, showing where this form of information diverges from expectation, baseline, prior state, or ideal alignment under boundary evaluation.

noncomps.name: Agentive_Operator_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Defines how ownership, commitment, correction, and answerability operate within the target domain, shaping whether it is merely present or actively stood behind. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. In practical terms, this is the agency law acting directly on the existence domain, so it tells Aurora what information looks like when interpreted through a ownership / commitment lens.

noncomps.name: Agentive_Polarity_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Establishes a bias toward commitment versus withdrawal, determining whether the target domain tends to be owned, enacted, deferred, or disowned. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a polarity slot, it does not define the target domain itself; it defines the directional lean of that domain, biasing it toward one side of its available behavioral range before full activation or collapse occurs.

noncomps.name: Agentive_Magnitude_of_Existence
noncomps.semantic: Measures how strongly the target domain is owned, claimed, or carried as an accountable commitment. Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. noncomps.name: Agentive_Cost_of_Existence

noncomps.name: Agentive_Difference_of_Existence

Within Existence / Information, this concerns whether something can register as real, stay present, differentiate from null, and matter as a signal. As a difference slot, it marks drift, mismatch, contrast, or deviation, showing where this form of information diverges from expectation, baseline, prior state, or ideal alignment under agency evaluation.

noncomps.name: Existential_Operator_of_Temporal
noncomps.semantic: Defines which structures are real enough to qualify within the target domain, filtering out what is too unstable, too diffuse, or too invalid to count. In practical terms, this is the existence law acting directly on the temporal domain, so it tells Aurora what belief looks like when interpreted through a gatekeeping / admissibility lens.

Within Energetic / Purpose, this concerns whether something can be activated, directed, sustained, focused, and converted into purposeful expenditure. In practical terms, this is the agency law acting directly on the energetic domain, so it tells Aurora what purpose looks like when interpreted through a ownership / commitment lens.

noncomps.name: Agentive_Polarity_of_Energetic
noncomps.semantic: Establishes a bias toward commitment versus withdrawal, determining whether the target domain tends to be owned, enacted, deferred, or disowned. Within Energetic / Purpose, this concerns whether something can be activated, directed, sustained, focused, and converted into purposeful expenditure. As a polarity slot, it does not define the target domain itself; it defines the directional lean of that domain, biasing it toward one side of its available behavioral range before full activation or collapse occurs.

noncomps.name: Agentive_Magnitude_of_Energetic
noncomps.semantic: Measures how strongly the target domain is owned, claimed, or carried as an accountable commitment. Within Energetic / Purpose, this concerns whether something can be activated, directed, sustained, focused, and converted into purposeful expenditure. As a magnitude slot, it represents load, amplitude, or strength rather than identity, telling Aurora how intense this form of purpose currently is when shaped by agency influence.

noncomps.name: Agentive_Cost_of_Energetic
noncomps.semantic: Captures the burden of ownership, commitment, and correction required for the target domain to be answerable. Within Energetic / Purpose, this concerns whether something can be activated, directed, sustained, focused, and converted into purposeful expenditure. As a cost slot, it expresses burden, drag, sacrifice, or maintenance price, showing what must be spent for this form of purpose to hold or function under agency pressure.

noncomps.name: Agentive_Difference_of_Energetic
noncomps.semantic: Captures the gap between what the target domain implies should be owned and what is actually carried, enacted, or corrected. Within Energetic / Purpose, this concerns whether something can be activated, directed, sustained, focused, and converted into purposeful expenditure. As a difference slot, it marks drift, mismatch, contrast, or deviation, showing where this form of purpose diverges from expectation, baseline, prior state, or ideal alignment under agency evaluation.

noncomps.name: Existential_Operator_of_Boundary
noncomps.semantic: Defines which structures are real enough to qualify within the target domain, filtering out what is too unstable, too diffuse, or too invalid to count. Within Boundary / Meaning, this concerns whether something can form coherent distinction, maintain contextual fit, preserve relation, and survive as a meaningful contour. In practical terms, this is the existence law acting directly on the boundary domain, so it tells Aurora what meaning looks like when interpreted through a gatekeeping / admissibility lens.

noncomps.name: Existential_Polarity_of_Boundary
noncomps.semantic: Establishes a bias toward valid presence versus non-presence, determining whether the target domain leans toward admissibility or suppression. Within Boundary / Meaning, this concerns whether something can form coherent distinction, maintain contextual fit, preserve relation, and survive as a meaningful contour. As a polarity slot, it does not define the target domain itself; it defines the directional lean of that domain, biasing it toward one side of its available behavioral range before full activation or collapse occurs.

noncomps.name: Existential_Magnitude_of_Boundary
noncomps.semantic: Measures how strongly the target domain presents itself as real and admissible. Within Boundary / Meaning, this concerns whether something can form coherent distinction, maintain contextual fit, preserve relation, and survive as a meaningful contour. As a magnitude slot, it represents load, amplitude, or strength rather than identity, telling Aurora how intense this form of meaning currently is when shaped by existence influence.

noncomps.name: Existential_Cost_of_Boundary
noncomps.semantic: Captures the price of making the target domain real enough to count at all. Within Boundary / Meaning, this concerns whether something can form coherent distinction, maintain contextual fit, preserve relation, and survive as a meaningful contour. As a cost slot, it expresses burden, drag, sacrifice, or maintenance price, showing what must be spent for this form of meaning to hold or function under existence pressure.

noncomps.name: Existential_Difference_of_Boundary
noncomps.semantic: Captures the gap between valid presence and invalid or unreal presence within the target domain. Within Boundary / Meaning, this concerns whether something can form coherent distinction, maintain contextual fit, preserve relation, and survive as a meaningful contour. As a difference slot, it marks drift, mismatch, contrast, or deviation, showing where this form of meaning diverges from expectation, baseline, prior state, or ideal alignment under existence evaluation.

noncomps.name: Temporal_Operator_of_Boundary
noncomps.semantic: Defines how structures in the target domain persist, sequence, decay, or continue, shaping whether they hold across time or collapse under transition. Within Boundary / Meaning, this concerns whether something can form coherent distinction, maintain contextual fit, preserve relation, and survive as a meaningful contour. In practical terms, this is the temporal law acting directly on the boundary domain, so it tells Aurora what meaning looks like when interpreted through a continuity / sequencing lens.
